{{Quote|Imperial Information Center, the great database on Coruscant.|Maizor|<ref name="Darksaber (novel)">[[Darksaber (novel)|''Darksaber'']]</ref>}}
The '''Imperial Information Center''' was a facility located deep within the bedrock of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], part of the [[Imperial Palace/Legends|Imperial Palace]] complex. It serviced the Palace of the Republic before it was inherited by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], and later the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].

==History==
Built into [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant's]] bedrock deep beneath the Palace of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]], the Information Center stored classified government documents, supplementing the Palace's data towers which erupted from its upper levels. Center staff consisted of human technicians and droids who processed millions of reports including political, military and economic. Staffers who worked there were subjected to the mind probes and loyalty conditioning. It was guarded by the elite Imperial Guards. When the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] rose to power, the Information Center began to house encrypted military files in addition to the centuries-old weather reports from the surface and other data such as [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] nightly dinner menu. While highly protected, infiltration was not impossible. [[Durga Besadii Tai|Durga]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] hired a slicer to gain access to the Center's databanks, stealing information that would go into the completion of the [[Darksaber (superweapon)|Darksaber Project]].<ref name="DS" />

When the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] ousted the Empire during the [[Liberation of Coruscant (Galactic Civil War)|liberation of Coruscant]], they took the Palace and preserved the Information Center because it contained important information on the planets of the former Empire and for intelligence operations. During the New Republic's occupation of the Palace, the [[Historical Council]] used files from the Center to write [[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']].<ref name="TEC">[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']]</ref>
